## ✅ Dignified Python Review

**Last updated:** 2026-02-24 17:40:21 PT

Found 0 violations across all Python files. All code changes comply with dignified-python standards.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Patterns Checked
✅ LBYL over EAFP - No try/except for control flow patterns
✅ Path operations - `.exists()` checks before `.resolve()` / `.is_relative_to()`
✅ Import organization - Proper module-level absolute imports, no re-exports
✅ Type syntax - Modern type annotations (e.g., `int | None`, `str | None`)
✅ Error handling - Proper error boundaries, no silent exception swallowing
✅ Properties - No O(n) properties or I/O in `@property`
✅ Frozen dataclasses - No violations detected
✅ Function signatures - All parameters properly organized
✅ Default parameters - No default parameter values in function signatures
✅ Keyword-only arguments - Multi-parameter functions properly use `*` separator

<!-- test-coverage-review -->

## ✅ Test Coverage Review

**Last updated:** 2026-02-24 13:04:57 PT

No violations detected. All source modifications have corresponding test coverage.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Analysis Summary

This PR is a refactoring that consolidates branch naming from legacy `P{issue}-` format to unified `plnd/` (planned-PR) format.

**Changes analyzed:**
- 30 source files modified (deletions, simplifications, and updates)
- 41 test files modified (assertions updated, functions tested)
- 1 test file deleted (tests for removed functions)

### Source File Coverage

| File Category | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **Naming functions** | ✅ | `generate_issue_branch_name()` and `extract_leading_issue_number()` deleted; tests in `test_naming.py` appropriately deleted (140 lines) |
| **Gateway branch operations** | ✅ | `get_branch_issue()` deprecated (returns None/None); covered in `test_fake_git.py` |
| **Plan data provider** | ✅ | Worktree mapping simplified; `_build_worktree_mapping()` changes covered in `test_plan_context_provider.py` |
| **CLI commands** | ✅ | Branch name generation updated; all CLI command tests updated with correct assertions |
| **Impl folder** | ✅ | `validate_plan_linkage()` simplified; covered in `test_impl_folder.py` |

### Test Coverage Assessment

- **No new functions without tests**: All modified functions have existing or deleted tests
- **Test deletions align with code deletions**: `test_naming.py` deleted because `extract_leading_issue_number()` and `generate_issue_branch_name()` were removed
- **Test updates comprehensive**: 41 test files updated to reflect new branch naming behavior
- **No test reduction with source additions**: All source modifications are simplifications/deletions, not additions

### Patterns Checked
✅ All modified functions have test coverage
✅ No new untested functions added
✅ Test files properly updated to reflect logic changes
✅ Deleted functions have corresponding test deletions

---

### Activity Log
- **2026-02-24 13:04:57 PT**: All source modifications have test coverage; test deletions align with function deletions; 41 test files updated with correct assertions

</details>

comments.body:
<!-- tripwires-review -->

## ⚠️ Tripwires Review

**Last updated:** 2026-02-24 17:40:13 PT

Found 1 issue requiring attention across 57 files.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Tripwires Triggered

The following tripwires were checked based on file patterns in the diff:

**Architecture Tripwires:**
- ✅ "adding a new method to Git ABC" → Not triggered (method deprecated, not added)
- ⚠️ **"modifying a gateway ABC method"** → TRIGGERED
  - **Doc loaded**: docs/learned/architecture/gateway-abc-implementation.md
  - **Rule**: When modifying a gateway ABC method, must update all 5 implementations (abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py, printing.py)

**Erk Tripwires:**
- ✅ "constructing branch names manually" → Checked, uses `generate_planned_pr_branch_name()`
- ✅ "trying to extract plan issue number from branch name" → Checked, properly removed

**Planning Tripwires:**
- ✅ "changing branch naming convention" → Checked, changes are consistent

**Universal Tripwires:**
- ✅ "using bare subprocess.run" → None found
- ✅ "calling os.chdir()" → None found
- ✅ "using Path.home()" → None found

### Tier 1 Pattern Matches (Mechanical)

✅ `subprocess\.run\(` - None found
✅ `os\.chdir\(` - None found  
✅ `Path\.home\(\)` - None found
✅ `\bimport time\b|time\.sleep\(|datetime\.now\(` - Only in deleted code
✅ `subprocess\.run\(\s*\[.*["']git` - None found outside gateways
✅ `monkeypatch\.|@patch` - Only in test code

### Tier 2 Semantic Matches (LLM-derived)

✅ **Adding new method to Git ABC** - No new methods added
✅ **Changing gateway method signatures** - Signature unchanged, only implementation/docstrings
⚠️ **Modifying gateway ABC method docstrings** - **Incomplete update detected**
✅ **Changing branch naming convention** - Changes consistent across codebase
✅ **Manual branch name construction** - All uses go through `generate_planned_pr_branch_name()`

### Issues Identified

**1. Incomplete gateway docstring update** (Cannot add inline comment - files not in diff)

**Files affected:**
- `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/branch_ops/dry_run.py:108`
- `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/branch_ops/printing.py:101`

**Issue:** The `get_branch_issue()` method docstrings in dry_run.py and printing.py still say "Extract GitHub issue number from branch name" but should be updated to "Deprecated: Branch names no longer encode issue numbers." to match the ABC, real, and fake implementations.

**Tripwire**: "modifying a gateway ABC method" → Read [Gateway ABC Implementation Checklist](docs/learned/architecture/gateway-abc-implementation.md) first. Must update all 5 implementations: abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py, printing.py.

**Why this matters**: Stale docstrings create confusion about the method's behavior. All 5 implementations should have consistent documentation when a method is deprecated or its behavior changes.

**Note**: The wrapper implementations themselves are correct (they delegate to the wrapped implementation), only the docstrings are stale.

### Files Reviewed

All 57 changed files were reviewed:
- Documentation files (docs/learned/): 11 files  
- Source code (packages/erk-shared/src/): 8 files
- Source code (src/erk/cli/): 7 files
- Test files (tests/): 30 files
- Skills/reference (.claude/): 1 file

---

### Activity Log
- **2026-02-24 17:40:13 PT**: Found 1 issue - stale docstrings in dry_run.py:108 and printing.py:101. Both still say "Extract GitHub issue number" but should say "Deprecated" to match abc/real/fake implementations. Implementation is correct (delegates to wrapped), only docstrings need update. Triggered tripwire: "modifying a gateway ABC method" → must update all 5 implementations.
- **2026-02-24 17:02:23 PT**: No tripwire violations detected. Reviewed branch naming convention cleanup (P{issue}- → plnd/). Verified all 3 gateway layers updated (abc/real/fake), tests updated alongside implementation (43 deleted, 21 added), documentation consistent. dry_run/printing wrappers delegate correctly.
- **2026-02-24 16:53:19 PT**: No tripwire violations detected. Reviewed branch naming convention cleanup (P{issue}- → plnd/). Verified all 3 gateway layers updated (abc/real/fake), tests updated alongside implementation, documentation consistent across all files.
- **2026-02-24 13:04:29 PT**: No tripwire violations detected. Reviewed branch naming convention cleanup (PXXXX → plnd/). All changes are documentation updates and implementation cleanups that deprecate extract_leading_issue_number() and update related code to use plan-ref.json as sole source of truth. Architecture changes properly update all 5 gateway layers (abc/real/fake/dry_run/printing where applicable).

</details>

comments.body:
<!-- dignified-code-simplifier-review -->

## ✅ Dignified Code Simplifier Review

**Last updated:** 2026-02-24 17:39:29 PT

No new violations detected in this comprehensive review.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Patterns Checked
✅ LBYL principle - All applied correctly
✅ Pathlib usage - Used consistently (Path.exists(), .resolve())
✅ Absolute imports - All correct, removed import of extract_leading_issue_number
✅ O(1) properties - Compliant (no I/O in properties/magic methods)
✅ Code clarity - Improvements made through dead code removal
✅ Conditional expressions - None violate nesting rules
✅ Unused imports - Removed appropriately
✅ Function signature clarity - Maintained
✅ Frozen dataclasses - All frozen decorators preserved
✅ Docstring clarity - Updated appropriately for deprecated methods

### Violations Summary
None detected. All changes follow dignified-python standards.

### Files Reviewed - Python Code

#### Core Modules
- `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/impl_folder.py`: Clean - LBYL pattern maintained, simplified validate_plan_linkage logic
- `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/naming.py`: Clean - extract_leading_issue_number removed entirely (dead code elimination)
- `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/issue_workflow.py`: Clean - Docstring example updated to plnd/ format
- `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/plan_data_provider/real.py`: Clean - Unused import removed, simplified _build_worktree_mapping

#### Gateway Layer
- `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/branch_ops/abc.py`: Clean - Docstring deprecation notice added clearly
- `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/branch_ops/fake.py`: Clean - Simplified correctly, fallback parsing logic removed, LBYL dict.get() used
- `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/branch_ops/real.py`: Clean - Simplified to return None, removed import and logic

#### Plan Store
- `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/plan_store/backend.py`: Clean - Docstring example updated
- `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/plan_store/github.py`: Clean - resolve_plan_id_for_branch simplified to return None, unused import removed

#### CLI Commands & Scripts
- `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/detect_plan_from_branch.py`: Clean - Docstring updated, logic simplified correctly
- `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/exit_plan_mode_hook.py`: Clean - Example updated
- `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/get_closing_text.py`: Clean - Docstring simplified
- `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/get_learn_sessions.py`: Clean - Docstring updated
- `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/handle_no_changes.py`: Clean - PR title format changed from P{id} to #{id}
- `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/objective_fetch_context.py`: Clean
- `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/track_learn_evaluation.py`: Clean - Docstring updated
- `src/erk/cli/commands/implement.py`: Clean - Error messages clarified for plan-ref.json
- `src/erk/cli/commands/implement_shared.py`: Clean - Docstring updated
- `src/erk/cli/commands/land_pipeline.py`: Clean - Unused import removed, simplified to set plan_id = None
- `src/erk/cli/commands/learn/learn_cmd.py`: Clean - Docstring updated
- `src/erk/cli/commands/plan/checkout_cmd.py`: Clean - Changed from extract_leading_issue_number to read_plan_ref pattern

#### Test Files
- `tests/commands/implement/test_issue_mode.py`: Clean - Test renamed and updated to reflect new behavior
- `tests/commands/land/test_get_objective_for_branch.py`: Clean - Tests simplified, unused helper functions removed
- `tests/commands/land/test_learn_skip_remote.py`: Clean - Test expectations updated
- `tests/commands/land/test_plan_issue_closure.py`: Clean - Tests significantly simplified, unused StatefulFakeGitHubIssues removed
- `tests/commands/objective/test_*.py`: All clean
- `tests/commands/pr/test_*.py`: All clean
- `tests/core/test_*.py`: All clean
- `tests/integration/plan_store/test_*.py`: All clean
- `tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_*.py`: All clean
- `tests/unit/cli/commands/test_*.py`: All clean
- `tests/unit/fakes/test_*.py`: All clean
- `tests/tui/data/test_*.py`: All clean

### Code Quality Notes

**Strengths:**
- Consistent removal of legacy `extract_leading_issue_number()` usage across all modules
- Proper LBYL checks maintained throughout (no EAFP patterns introduced)
- Dead code elimination handled cleanly without leaving orphaned code paths
- Docstrings updated consistently to reflect new behavior and deprecation
- No unnecessary abstractions introduced
- Branch naming simplification achieved without introducing code smell
- Import cleanup executed systematically
- Test suite updated to match new expectations without losing coverage

**Patterns Applied Correctly:**
- Frozen dataclasses maintained (IssueBranchSetup)
- Absolute imports enforced (no relative imports added)
- Path operations use pathlib.Path consistently with .exists() checks
- Error handling uses discriminated unions (PRNotFound type checking)
- LBYL pattern used correctly: `self._branch_issues.get(branch)` instead of try/except
- Direct return of None instead of unnecessary conditionals

### Documentation Changes
Documentation changes (branch-naming.md, planning/one-shot-workflow.md, etc.) are outside the scope of code simplification review. These are addressed by separate documentation review processes.

---

### Activity Log
- **2026-02-24 17:39:29 PT**: Fifth review - Comprehensive final verification. No violations detected. Code changes consistently follow dignified-python standards including LBYL, pathlib usage, absolute imports, and dead code removal patterns.
- **2026-02-24 17:02:38 PT**: Fourth review - No violations detected. Code quality consistent with dignified-python standards throughout.
- **2026-02-24 16:53:45 PT**: Third review - Comprehensive final pass on all Python files. No violations detected. Code quality consistent with dignified-python standards throughout.
- **2026-02-24 13:04:49 PT**: Second review - verified all changes follow dignified-python standards. No violations detected.
- **2026-02-24 11:40:52 PT**: Initial review - Found 1 redundant conditional in impl_folder.py:308 (unnecessary `if impl_dir.exists()` check before `read_plan_ref()`)

</details>
